title: Living with extreme weather
explanation:
Living with extreme weather

In recent years, many regions have experienced more frequent extreme weather events, including floods, heatwaves and severe storms. Scientists say that while no single event can be explained by climate change alone, long-term trends make some events more likely and more intense. As a result, cities are beginning to rethink how they are built. Some are planting more trees to lower temperatures, while others are improving drainage systems to reduce flood damage. Adapting to extreme weather is expensive, but many experts believe the cost of doing nothing would be far greater.

questions.text: Recent years have brought more frequent [*extreme weather events|music festivals|power cuts only].
questions.feedback: The passage opens with this idea.

questions.text: Climate change may make some events more [*likely and intense|short and cheap|easy to predict].
questions.feedback: The article says more likely and more intense.

questions.text: Some cities are planting more trees to lower [*temperatures|rent|traffic].
Experts think doing nothing would be [*more costly|safer|more relaxing].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: One adaptation strategy is improving city ____ systems. | *drainage | lecture | refund
No single weather event can be explained by climate change ____.  | *alone | therefore | instead
questions.feedback: That exact idea appears in the text.

questions.text: Heatwaves, storms and floods are all examples of extreme weather ____.  | *events | audiences | products
Adapting to these changes can be very ____.  | *expensive | formal | impossible
questions.feedback: The text says it is expensive.

questions.text: The passage is mainly about [*responding to climate-related risks|farming methods|tourism in cities].
The writer's attitude is [*serious but practical|joking|uncertain about the existence of weather].
questions.feedback: The tone is serious and solution-focused.

questions.text: The article suggests cities are [*already taking action|refusing to change|reducing public services].
questions.feedback: They are rethinking how they are built.

questions.text: The text contrasts the cost of adaptation with the cost of [*doing nothing|tree planting|new housing].
